mission.title: Install and Configure MySQL Operator for Kubernetes (Oracle)
mission.description: The MySQL Operator for Kubernetes automates the management of MySQL InnoDB Cluster setups within a Kubernetes environment. It simplifies tasks such as deployment, scaling, and backup, making it ideal for users who need a robust database solution in Kubernetes. Key trade-offs include the complexity of operator management versus the benefits of automated lifecycle management.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the MySQL Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the operator using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add mysql-operator https://mysql.github.io/mysql-operator/

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official MySQL Operator repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install MySQL Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the MySQL Operator in a dedicated namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install mysql-operator mysql-operator/mysql-operator --namespace mysql-operator --create-namespace --version 9.0.0

```

> Installs the MySQL Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify Operator Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the MySQL Operator is running correctly in the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl get deployment -n mysql-operator mysql-operator

```

> You should see the operator deployment status.

steps.title: Create MySQL Secret
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes secret containing the MySQL root user credentials needed for the InnoDB Cluster.

```bash

kubectl create secret generic mypwds --from-literal=rootUser=root --from-literal=rootHost=% --from-literal=rootPassword: <REDACTED>'

```

> Replace 'sakila' with a secure password.

steps.title: Define MySQL InnoDB Cluster
steps.description:
This step defines a MySQL InnoDB Cluster resource in the Kubernetes cluster using a YAML manifest.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: mysql.oracle.com/v2
kind: InnoDBCluster
metadata:
  name: mycluster
spec:
  secretName: mypwds
  version: '8.0.26'
EOF

```

> This creates an InnoDB Cluster named 'mycluster'.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: mysql.oracle.com/v2
kind: InnoDBCluster
metadata:
  name: mycluster
spec:
  secretName: mypwds
  version: '8.0.26'
EOF

steps.title: Verify InnoDB Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks if the InnoDB Cluster is created and running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get innodbclusters.mysql.oracle.com mycluster

```

> You should see the status of the InnoDB Cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MySQL Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the MySQL Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall mysql-operator --namespace mysql-operator

kubectl delete namespace mysql-operator

```

> This command uninstalls the MySQL Operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MySQL Operator
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up existing resources, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get innodbclusters.mysql.oracle.com mycluster -o yaml > mycluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade mysql-operator mysql-operator/mysql-operator --namespace mysql-operator --version 9.0.1

kubectl get deployment -n mysql-operator mysql-operator

```

> Backup the current InnoDB Cluster configuration.
> Upgrade to the new version of the MySQL Operator.
> Verify that the operator is running the new version.
commands: kubectl get innodbclusters.mysql.oracle.com mycluster -o yaml > mycluster-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create InnoDB Cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MySQL Operator may not have sufficient permissions to create resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure the operator has the necessary RBAC permissions configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Secret not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified secret for MySQL credentials does not exist.

**Fix:**
Create the secret using the correct command with valid credentials.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'MySQL Operator not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The operator deployment may have failed due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs for errors and ensure sufficient resources are allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'InnoDB Cluster not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The InnoDB Cluster may be in the process of initializing or encountering issues.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the InnoDB Cluster and the operator logs for detailed error messages.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for MySQL 8.0.26, improved backup and restore functionalities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the InnoDB Cluster status as 'Ready' when everything is working: `kubectl get innodbclusters.mysql.oracle.com mycluster` should show `STATUS: Ready`.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: mysql.oracle.com/v2
kind: InnoDBCluster
metadata:
  name: mycluster
spec:
  secretName: mypwds
  version: '8.0.26'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
